title: oxyacantha howellii
intro: Oxyacantha howellii, also known as Howell's hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a type of thorny shrub or small tree that is native to the southeastern United States and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its cardiotonic and vasodilatory effects. The plant contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular issues, such as high blood pressure and angina, as well as to promote overall cardiovascular health and well-being. Oxyacantha howellii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support heart health and reduce the risk of heart disease.
